note: Verifies whether Biome 2.5.4's type-aware lint rules fire (pass/fail on diagnostic presence), with `typescript` NOT installed. No statistical distribution applies: fixed fixtures produce fixed diagnostics for a pinned Biome build.

behaviors_verified.claim: A type-aware rule (noFloatingPromises) distinguishes a discarded Promise from an awaited one with NO typescript installed — Biome infers the return type itself.
behaviors_verified.value: bare save() flagged; await save() not flagged; node_modules/typescript absent

behaviors_verified.claim: The floating-promise check works ACROSS files: a function's Promise return type, known only via import, is still resolved by Biome's project scanner.
behaviors_verified.value: mod-b.ts bare fetchUser(1) flagged; project-wide scan indexes mod-a's signature

behaviors_verified.claim: Biome's own inference is not a full type system. It flags native Promise-returning calls and Promise.all aggregates, but MISSES a hand-rolled PromiseLike thenable that typescript-eslint would catch — a concrete slice of the ~75% coverage gap.
behaviors_verified.value: flagged lines = {3 native Promise, 11 Promise.all}; line 8 custom thenable NOT flagged

behaviors_verified.claim: A custom lint rule can be authored as a single declarative .grit query — no Rust/JS rule compilation — and it fires with category `plugin`.
behaviors_verified.value: Object.assign({}, a, {y:2}) flagged with the custom message; category plugin

cited_not_measured.claim: Biome's type-aware rules cover roughly 75% of typescript-eslint's type-aware rule set.
Biome lints ~10-20x faster than ESLint on comparable projects.
cited_not_measured.source: Biome docs / v2 announcement (machine-dependent, NOT measured in this run)

cited_not_measured.claim: noFloatingPromises remains in the nursery group (opt-in) as of 2.5.4.
